skill.name: state-management
skill.description: Guides React state management using TanStack Query, Zustand, React Hook Form, Zod, and URL state patterns.
skill.summary: Apply practical React patterns for server, client, form, and URL state.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static external command, system reconnaissance, and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, JavaScript examples, and frontmatter text. Semantic review found one moderate issue: the Zustand reference encourages persisting an auth token in sessionStorage, which can increase credential exposure after XSS.

medium_findings.title: Client-Side Auth Token Persistence Example
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The Zustand reference stores an auth token in sessionStorage through persist middleware. This is not malicious code, but copying the pattern can expose bearer tokens to client-side script compromise and should be avoided or clearly warned.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example explicitly defines a token field and persists it through sessionStorage. The risk depends on application context, but the insecure storage pattern is clear.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static external_commands findings correspond to Markdown TypeScript code fences, not Ruby shell backtick execution. No executable shell command, process spawn, or command interpolation was found in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line is a Markdown code fence delimiter or adjacent documentation boundary. The files contain examples only and no executable script entry point.

low_findings.title: Blocker Alerts Are Benign State Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static system reconnaissance alerts match normal JavaScript state-management examples, such as enabled guards, array find calls, and store access. No command that inspects the host system was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary frontend code examples and do not call operating system tools. The surrounding context is state querying and component rendering.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Frontmatter Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the skill description frontmatter. That line only describes TanStack Query and Zustand patterns and contains no cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text. There is no hash function, cipher, crypto import, or algorithm selection at that location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Auth Token Stored in sessionStorage
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference pattern persists an auth token in browser sessionStorage. This can turn an XSS issue into token theft and should be replaced with safer authentication storage guidance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The storage mechanism and persisted token field are both visible in the example. The security impact is well understood for browser token storage.

content.value_statement: React apps often mix server, client, form, and URL state without clear ownership. This skill provides concise patterns for TanStack Query, Zustand, React Hook Form, Zod, and URL state.
actual_capabilities: Selects the right state layer for server, client, form, and URL state.
Provides TanStack Query key factories, query hooks, mutation hooks, and invalidation patterns.
Shows Zustand store typing, selectors, persistence middleware, and slice composition.
Demonstrates React Hook Form with Zod validation for typed form data.
Includes reference examples for prefetching, infinite queries, optimistic updates, and computed selectors.
limitations: Does not execute code, install packages, or inspect a live application by itself.
Does not cover every TanStack Query or Zustand API option.
Includes a token persistence example that needs security review before production use.
Assumes a React and TypeScript project context.

use_cases.title: Plan State Boundaries
use_cases.description: Map app state to TanStack Query, Zustand, React Hook Form, Zod, or URL parameters before implementation.

use_cases.title: Refactor Data Fetching
use_cases.description: Convert ad hoc fetch logic into typed query hooks, stable query keys, and predictable cache invalidation.

use_cases.title: Review Store Performance
use_cases.description: Check Zustand usage for selector-based reads, focused stores, and avoidable component re-renders.

prompt_templates.title: Choose State Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React feature and tell me which state belongs in TanStack Query, Zustand, React Hook Form, Zod, or the URL.

prompt_templates.title: Create Query Hooks
prompt_templates.prompt: Design TanStack Query keys and hooks for this resource. Include list, detail, create, and update flows.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Zustand Store
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Zustand store and suggest typed selectors, slices, and persistence changes that reduce re-renders.

prompt_templates.title: Audit State Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this React state architecture for cache ownership, mutation invalidation, form validation, persistence risks, and URL state boundaries.

output_examples.input: A users page fetches lists, opens edit forms, and tracks a sidebar setting.
output_examples.output: Recommended split: TanStack Query owns user lists and detail records, React Hook Form owns edit input, Zod validates submissions, and Zustand owns the sidebar preference.

output_examples.input: A dashboard re-renders after any Zustand store update.
output_examples.output: Use focused selectors for each component, split unrelated state into slices, and avoid reading the full store in render paths.

output_examples.input: A mutation updates a user but stale list data remains visible.
output_examples.output: Invalidate the detail query for that user and the users list query after success. Consider optimistic updates only when rollback behavior is defined.

best_practices: Keep server state in TanStack Query and avoid duplicating it in Zustand.
Use narrow Zustand selectors so components subscribe only to needed values.
Validate form data with Zod before mutation calls or cache updates.
anti_patterns: Do not store sensitive auth tokens in browser persistence without a security review.
Do not read an entire Zustand store when a component needs one field.
Do not use unstable query keys that change shape between renders.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete application code?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that can guide implementation. It does not create a full app by itself.

faq.question: Which React state library does it prefer?
faq.answer: It uses TanStack Query for server state and Zustand for client state. Forms and URL values have separate owners.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use typed query keys, typed stores, and Zod-inferred form data.

faq.question: Is the persistence guidance production safe?
faq.answer: Most persistence examples are ordinary preferences. The auth token example needs security review before production use.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it improve React performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes Zustand selectors and focused subscriptions to reduce unnecessary re-renders.
